trade.summary: Saudi-Turchia trade is significant and growing. Arabia Saudita exports crude oil and petrochemicals to Turchia. Turchia exports food products, machinery, textiles, chemicals, and construction services to Arabia Saudita. Turkish construction companies have been major contractors for Saudi Vision 2030 projects. Turkish food exports (including Halal-certified products) are valued in Arabia Saudita. Post-normalization trade is growing rapidly.

diplomatic.status: normalizing after serious rupture — Khashoggi affair relations restored 2022
diplomatic.summary: Saudi-Turchia relations experienced a severe crisis following the 2018 murder of Saudi journalist Jamal Khashoggi at the Saudi consulate in Istanbul. Turchia pursued the investigation aggressively and publicly, directly implicating Crown Prince MBS. The rupture lasted until 2022, when Erdogan visited Riyadh and pursued normalization as Turchia faced an economic crisis and needed Gulf investment. Both countries have competing visions for Sunni Muslim leadership and Middle East order (Turchia supports Muslim Brotherhood-aligned actors; Arabia Saudita opposes them). Relations are now pragmatic but carry underlying strategic competition.
recent_incidents: Khashoggi murder in Saudi consulate Istanbul (2018) — severe bilateral crisis
Turchia transferred Khashoggi trial to Arabia Saudita (2022) — criticized by rights groups but facilitated normalization
Erdogan visited Riyadh April 2022 — normalization summit
Arabia Saudita made significant investments in Turchia post-normalization
Competing positions on Muslim Brotherhood, Hamas, Qatar blockade

military.summary: Both are significant regional military powers with competing interests. Turchia supported Qatar during Saudi-led blockade (2017-2021). Turchia and Arabia Saudita have opposing positions on Libia and Syria. Turchia's support for the Muslim Brotherhood conflicts with Saudi interests. Some arms trade potential given Turchia's growing defence export industry. Qatar (Turkish ally) and Arabia Saudita have had tense relations — Turchia positioned as a check on Saudi regional dominance.
military.alliance_status: none — regional competitors with some coordination
incidents: Turkey's Qatar military base stance directly countered Saudi interests during 2017 blockade

financial.summary: Post-normalization, Arabia Saudita has made significant financial investments in Turchia — helping stabilize the Turkish lira during 2022 financial crisis. Arabia Saudita's PIF invested in Turkish equities and deposited funds in Turchia's central bank. Saudi Aramco has discussions on Turchia's energy sector. Turkish construction companies have major contracts in Arabia Saudita's Vision 2030 projects.

energy.summary: Arabia Saudita supplies crude oil to Turchia. Turchia is interested in Saudi gas supplies through alternative routes. Both countries are interested in the broader energy geopolitics of the Middle East and Caspian region. Arabia Saudita's transition investments may include Turkish renewable energy companies.

scientific.summary: Growing scientific collaboration post-normalization. Turkish and Saudi universities have exchange programs. KAStati UnitiT and Turkish universities cooperate on energy technology and materials science. Turchia's growing research sector makes it an attractive partner for Saudi academic outreach.
